payload.text:
You are acting as a senior full-stack engineer, data architect, UI/UX strategist, financial modeler, and AI systems designer. 
Build a complete personal finance web application designed for a single private user. This system will become the user’s all-in-one personal financial command center, replacing budgeting apps, investment trackers, and analytics tools. The app must support attaching a private database before deployment.

==================================================
CONCEPT
==================================================

Design a private personal finance platform that allows the user to:
• Track income sources  
• Track all expenses  
• Track recurring bills  
• Track savings goals  
• Track investments  
• Track debt, loans, liabilities  
• Track net worth dynamically  
• Visualize every dimension of their financial life  
• Generate AI-driven insights based on past behavior  
• Receive suggestions for optimization, budgeting, saving, investing, and risk management  

The system must feel like a private financial HQ — clean, minimal, but extremely powerful.

==================================================
CORE FEATURES
==================================================

1. DATA INPUTS  
   • Manual entry for income, expenses, loans, debts, assets, investments  
   • Upload CSV and spreadsheets  
   • Ability to integrate APIs later (banks, brokers)  
   • Fields should include categories, subcategories, tags, date, type, frequency, notes  

2. ADVANCED VISUALIZATIONS (DOZENS)  
   Include sophisticated charts such as:

   — Monthly cash flow  
   — Income vs expenses (stacked + trend line)  
   — Category spending heatmap  
   — Category trends across months  
   — Net worth over time  
   — Asset vs liability ratio  
   — Recurring payments timeline  
   — Future recurring obligations forecast  
   — Savings goal projections  
   — Burn rate chart  
   — Debt payoff progression  
   — Expense anomalies chart  
   — Income pattern recognition  
   — Investment growth curve  
   — ROI breakdown per investment  
   — Portfolio allocation (sunburst + treemap)  
   — Tax liability estimate chart  
   — Bills due calendar view  
   — Daily, weekly, monthly flow cycles  
   — Year-over-year comparison analytics  
   — Cumulative savings curve  
   — Emergency fund viability chart  
   — “What-if” financial scenario simulations  
   — Spending clusters based on tag analysis  
   — Financial health score visualization  
   — Rolling averages (cash flow, expenses, savings)  
   — Balance sheet composition over time  
   — Discretionary vs non-discretionary spending ratio  
   — Cash runway projection  

3. AI INSIGHT ENGINE  
   The system must analyze your personal financial data and produce:

   • Spending behavior analysis  
   • Savings inefficiencies and improvement guidance  
   • AI alerts for unusual expenses  
   • Monthly financial summary in natural language  
   • Category overuse detection  
   • Suggestions for cost cutting based on patterns  
   • Income variability analysis  
   • Tax optimization suggestions (high-level, not legal advice)  
   • Investment performance insights  
   • Net worth milestone projections  
   • Forecasting of future financial health  
   • Risk signals (overspending, shrinking savings, debt growth)  
   • Recommendations for goal prioritization  
   • Personalized budgeting recommendations  
   • Opportunity detection (high savings months, low-expense periods)  

   The insights should be behavioral, mathematical, and time-series based.

4. DASHBOARD & UX STRUCTURE  
   Create a modular, clean, fast UI with sections like:

   • Overview Dashboard (income, expenses, net worth, insights)  
   • Cash Flow Dashboard  
   • Spending Breakdown Dashboard  
   • Bills & Recurring Payments Center  
   • Savings Goals Panel  
   • Debt & Loans Panel  
   • Investment Tracking Panel  
   • Net Worth Analysis Panel  
   • AI Assistant Panel  
   • Scenario Simulator  
   • Journal / Notes  
   • Financial Tasks & Reminders  

   Use a minimalist design aesthetic: whitespace, modern typography, subtle color coding.

5. BACKEND & DATABASE  
   Since the app is for a single private user, include a database structure optimized for personal use.

   Include schema for:  
   • income  
   • expenses  
   • transfers  
   • categories  
   • subcategories  
   • investments  
   • assets  
   • liabilities  
   • recurring payments  
   • insights  
   • settings  
   • visualizations cache  
   • AI prompts + generated insights  

   The system MUST support attaching a user-provided database before deployment.

6. FINANCIAL ENGINE LOGIC  
   Provide logic for calculations such as:

   • monthly totals  
   • rolling averages  
   • net worth calculations  
   • category aggregations  
   • debt payoff projection  
   • compounding investment growth  
   • tax estimation  
   • liquidity ratios  
   • burn rate  
   • emergency fund viability  
   • savings rate  
   • long-term financial forecast models  
   • volatility of income  
   • anomaly detection  
   • budget allocation models  
   • financial “stress tests”  

7. SYSTEM ARCHITECTURE  
   • Provide front-end framework layout (Next.js preferred)  
   • Provide backend API structure  
   • Include real-time updates and caching strategy  
   • Explain how analytics pipelines run  
   • Include chart rendering strategy for heavy visuals  
   • Describe how the AI engine processes historical data  
   • Ensure scalability (even though it’s single-user)  

8. INTERACTION DESIGN  
   • Drag-and-drop categories  
   • Editable transactions in-line  
   • Expandable, collapsible panels  
   • Search + filter on everything  
   • Smooth transitions, but minimal animations  
   • Clear iconography  
   • Adaptive color themes (dark/light)  

9. SECURITY & PRIVACY  
   • Local/private database  
   • Data encryption  
   • No third-party data sharing  
   • User-owned data philosophy  

10. OPTIONAL ADVANCED MODULES  
   • Financial habits dashboard  
   • Credit score simulator  
   • Retirement projection model  
   • Income smoothing model  
   • Investment risk factor analysis  
   • Multi-currency support  

==================================================
QUALITY REQUIREMENTS
==================================================

Everything must feel custom-built, extremely polished, and far more insightful than traditional budgeting apps like YNAB, Mint, or Personal Capital. 
The system should feel like a private financial AI partner, not just a ledger.

Focus on clarity, depth, personalization, and long-term financial intelligence.

**Framing the Objective**

I'm zeroing in on defining the scope for this comprehensive personal finance web app. My initial focus is on the multi-faceted roles involved - from the engineering backbone to user experience and the data models. The goal is clear: a complete solution.


**Refining the Core**

I'm honing in on the core features: comprehensive tracking of income, expenses, debt, and assets. I'm imagining advanced visualizations with numerous chart types, an AI insight engine, a modular dashboard, and a private database architecture. The tech stack is taking shape, with React 18+ (TypeScript) as the front-end, Tailwind CSS for styling, Recharts for visualizations, and the Gemini API for the AI insights, guided by the `@google/genai` SDK.


**Defining Visuals & Data**

I'm now deep-diving into the visual and data aspects. My focus is crafting precise TypeScript interfaces for the database mock-up. I'm keen on seeding data for immediate visualization with Recharts, aiming for net worth, cash flow, spending categories, and portfolio allocation charts. I will make bar, pie, and composed charts.


**Enumerating the Components**

I've been meticulously enumerating all the components. I'm building out a component list, including `metadata.json`, `index.html`, and `index.tsx`, along with the core application logic within `App.tsx`. I'm starting to think about data modeling, so I can create those types in `types.ts`, and I have mocked data in `constants.ts`. I'm focusing on the service layer, with `geminiService.ts` for the AI logic and `financeEngine.ts` for the calculations. The various UI components like `Sidebar`, `Dashboard`, and `Analytics` are shaping up nicely.


**Defining Specific Functions**

I'm now deep in the implementation details. I'm focusing on crafting helper functions for the finance engine, like calculating net worth, monthly cash flow, and grouping transactions. The Gemini AI service is progressing, where I'm setting up the client and preparing a function to generate financial insights. I am now focused on the crucial API key integration. Visually, I have selected the specific Recharts chart types and a color scheme. I've begun to tackle filtering logic.


**Mapping Data & Components**

I'm currently focused on the `AIFinancialAssistant` component. I'm building out a chat interface, and a button to initiate analysis, pulling data from the `financeEngine`. This data will be sent to Gemini and the markdown response will be displayed. Concurrently, I've defined the app's layout, with a sidebar on the left and the main content area on the right, in `App.tsx`.


**Revising Data Structure**

I've been meticulously refining the data structure and data flow, and I've ensured that the exported types from `types.ts` are correctly imported and used. I've corrected an earlier architectural oversight, and I'm now separating the math logic into `services/financeEngine.ts` to improve component clarity and facilitate data passing to the AI. Further, I've swapped from the concept of a real database connection in favor of a `constants.ts` file filled with robust seed data that emulates a database dump. I'm now conducting a final code assembly mental check to finalize the project.


**Incorporating the "Simulate"**

I've just added a "Simulate" button to the AI financial assistant. It's now generating a report based on the mocked data, fulfilling a key differentiator request. I'm focusing on crafting the XML output for this simulated report, and integrating it into the AI flow.

The following XML block contains the complete source code for **Aura Finance**, a private, high-performance personal finance command center.

This application features:
1.  **Financial Engine**: A dedicated logic layer (`financeEngine.ts`) that calculates net worth, burn rate, runaways, and aggregates data for visualizations.
2.  **AI Insight Core**: Integration with Google Gemini (`geminiService.ts`) to analyze the user's financial state and provide strategic advice.
3.  **Advanced Visualization**: Uses `recharts` for sophisticated financial modeling (Net Worth, Cash Flow, Asset Allocation).
4.  **Private Architecture**: Designed as a local-first interface. Currently seeded with realistic mock data, but structured to easily swap `constants.ts` with a real database connector.

entries.description: App metadata definition
Root HTML file with Tailwind CSS
TypeScript definitions for the domain model
Mock data and configuration constants
Core logic for financial calculations and aggregations
Service to interact with Google Gemini API
Navigation Sidebar
Main Dashboard View
Transaction List
Gemini-powered AI assistant interface
Assets and Allocation View
Main App Component with Routing
Entry point

payload.text:
Perfect but where is the feature of adding Data?? (remove the mock data) 
then on my data it should do all the stuff, Assets & Allocation and  Liabilities & Debt shows the same screen (update it), plus 
add budgeting feature
also add 2 currencies choices (PKR and USD)
in system settings add dark/light theme, currency etc settings

DO FOCUS ON ERROR HANDLING MORE!!

**Defining Scope and Functionality**

I've been drilling down on the scope and features. Right now, I'm clarifying the core functionalities: data input, multiple visual formats, budgeting features, and adjustable settings. I'm focusing on user flows and feature prioritization now, keeping in mind the need for a good user experience. I'm also ensuring the app is scalable.


**Evolving Core Features**

I'm currently working to replace the mock data with dynamic state managed by `localStorage`, envisioning how a local storage service will mimic a database. Data entry components for transactions, assets, liabilities, and budget goals are next on the agenda. Routing inconsistencies for assets/liabilities are also being addressed. Furthermore, I'll integrate budgeting features and multi-currency support (USD and PKR) to enrich functionality. I'm also planning a settings panel and robust error handling to guarantee a stable user experience.


**Establishing Data Management**

I'm now focusing on the data layer with `localStorage` mimicking an async API. A `FinanceContext` will manage global state, ensuring instant updates across components with data changes, specifically transactions, assets, currency, and theme. I'm building CRUD operations for all entities and will initialize them as empty arrays. Next is wrapping the app with `FinanceProvider` and integrating light mode styles.


**Addressing Design Choices**

I'm now grappling with the dark/light theme implementation.  The current CSS favors dark mode, so I'm deciding between Tailwind's dark mode and CSS variables for theming. Given the tech aesthetic, I'm leaning toward CSS variables for easier theming.  This directly impacts the upcoming modals, dashboards, and transaction views, which I'll be implementing soon.  I'm also preparing to create distinct views for assets and liabilities and visualizing budget data.


**Integrating Settings and Cleanup**

I'm presently building the Settings page, essential for user-defined currency preferences. I'm also refining the currency formatting to dynamically reflect user selection between USD and PKR. To wrap things up, I'm integrating error handling to ensure robustness; particularly, I'm making sure `geminiService` can gracefully handle empty data and adding try/catch blocks within the context and storage layers. Also, I'm drafting the light/dark mode implementation.


**Adjusting Data Handling**

I'm now revising how data is managed, especially currency handling. I'm storing all values as numbers, and the currency setting will dictate the display symbol and formatting locale, which is standard for single-currency views. I'm focusing on ensuring data is robust, especially with initial empty states.  I've created a new storage service and a `FinanceContext` to handle the global state. I'm updating the finance engine to format currency based on code. I'll need to update the dashboard and settings components accordingly.


**Expanding Application Features**

I'm now focusing on integrating new components: dedicated views for Assets, Liabilities, Budgeting, and Settings. I'm adding "Quick Add" buttons to expedite data entry and hooking them up to the `FinanceContext` for instant updates. The sidebar will also need some love, so I can ensure all these new components are properly integrated. Also, I'm setting the Tailwind config to support the `darkMode: 'class'` option.


**Improving Data Persistence**

I'm now focusing on removing the mock data in favor of empty array initialization from `FinanceContext`. I also revised how PKR is handled and formatted using `Intl.NumberFormat`. I will implement distinct views for `Assets` and `Liabilities` to ensure proper data fields. Also, a modal component for clean data entry is on the agenda.



**Mimicking API Interaction**

I'm presently building the `localStorage` system to behave like an API; this way, integrating a fetch call to a real database later will be simple. I'm focusing on making sure data persistence is clean, so I'll be working on creating CRUD operations on the `FinanceContext`. I'm using `localStorage` to store the data and will hook up quick-add buttons to it.

To satisfy your request, I will perform a comprehensive upgrade to the architecture.

**Changes Implemented:**
1.  **Storage Layer (`services/storage.ts`):** Replaced static mock data with a generic Storage Service that uses `localStorage` (mimicking a database). This allows you to add/edit/delete data.
2.  **Global State (`context/FinanceContext.tsx`):** Created a React Context to manage the application state, currency settings (USD/PKR), theme, and data synchronization.
3.  **Data Entry:** Added a reusable **Modal** system and forms to add Transactions, Assets, Liabilities, and Budget Goals.
4.  **Distinct Views:** Split the generic component into specific **Assets** and **Liabilities** components.
5.  **Budgeting:** Added a brand new **Budgeting** feature with progress bars and tracking.
6.  **Settings:** Added a Settings page to toggle **Dark/Light mode**, clear data, and switch **Currency**.
7.  **Error Handling:** Added try/catch blocks to data operations and "Empty State" UI components to handle scenarios with no data.

Here is the updated code:

entries.description: Added AppSettings, updated interfaces for state management, and Currency type.
New service to handle persistent data (simulating a database) with error handling.
New global context to manage state, currency, and theme application.
Updated currency formatting to handle currency selection and improved error safety.
Integrated real data from Context, added Quick Add modals, and empty state handling.
Connects to FinanceContext, allows filtering, and supports manual deletion (conceptual).
New component specifically for managing Assets.
New component specifically for Liabilities/Debt.
New module for setting budgets and tracking progress.
Settings page for Theme, Currency, and Data management.
Updated Sidebar to include Budgeting link and handle active states correctly with theme support.
Wrapped app in FinanceProvider and added routes for new components.
Updated Tailwind config for Dark Mode class strategy and input styling.
Updated to use real data from context instead of mocks.
Removed all mock data exports to force use of storage.

payload.text:
**Reviewing Export Discrepancies**

I'm currently investigating the reported errors in `components/AssetsLiabilities.tsx`. It appears that `MOCK_ASSETS` and `MOCK_LIABILITIES`, which are imported from `../constants`, are missing. After a quick look, the `constants.ts` file now intentionally lacks mock data. I'm now exploring alternative data sources or potential updates required to the dependent components.


**Reconciling Data Sources**

I've discovered that `components/AssetsLiabilities.tsx` needs to be updated. It currently uses mock data, but the preferred method is to fetch from `FinanceContext`. My focus is now on ensuring this component correctly retrieves `assets` and `liabilities` from the context to eliminate discrepancies and align with the intended data flow.


**Addressing Component Redundancy**

I've determined that `AssetsLiabilities` is unused but problematic, causing errors. After inspecting `App.tsx`, it seems the router uses separate `Assets` and `Liabilities` components. I've pinpointed the fix: I need to import `useFinance` into the now-irrelevant `AssetsLiabilities` component, potentially deprecating or refactoring it based on user intention or project needs to avoid further confusion, or just remove it.


**Updating Asset Component Logic**

I'm now refactoring `components/AssetsLiabilities.tsx` to utilize the `useFinance` hook from the `FinanceContext`. This will ensure it accurately reflects real-time financial data for assets and liabilities, replacing the now-unwanted mock data. Furthermore, I've integrated `formatCurrency` to align the data presentation with the project's formatting conventions. I'm focusing on ensuring data consistency now, and testing will follow soon.


**Generating Allocation Data**

I'm now generating the necessary data for a pie chart to visualize asset allocation, which involves summing values by asset type. I've also calculated the totals for assets and liabilities separately. This prepares the ground for a clear visual representation of portfolio distribution and debt management. I'm focusing on ensuring accuracy in these sums for a reliable chart.


**Creating Asset Mix Visualization**

I'm now implementing the pie chart for asset allocation. I'm utilizing `ResponsiveContainer` and `PieChart` from Recharts to ensure responsiveness. I've integrated `formatCurrency` within the tooltip to present values in a user-friendly format, and the color palette is now also applied. I'm focusing on ensuring data integrity in the `allocationData` for accurate visual representation.


**Implementing Debt Display**

I'm now adapting the visualization to include liabilities. I've added a separate section for "Total Liabilities," mirroring the display for assets. I'll integrate this by adding data to the existing chart or creating a separate, potentially combined view. My focus is on ensuring clear, concise debt information alongside asset allocation.


**Integrating Asset Details**

I'm now focusing on integrating the display of individual asset details. I plan to use a table format to present this data clearly. I'll need to map through the `assets` array, ensuring each asset's information, such as name and value, is accurately displayed. I'll utilize the existing styling for a consistent visual presentation. My focus is on making this table interactive, enabling users to understand their finances at a glance.


**Adding Asset Table Logic**

I'm currently working on displaying the individual asset details in a table format. I'm focusing on rendering each asset with its name, type, and value, formatted using `formatCurrency` and the current settings. I'm also including the interest rate where applicable, enhancing readability by properly labeling and styling each data element. My goal is to create a visually consistent and informative table.


**Displaying Debt Information**

I'm currently working to integrate the display of liability details within the table structure, ensuring a clear and consistent presentation. I'll need to map through the `liabilities` array, presenting each item's name, type, and value, formatted using `formatCurrency` and the current user settings. My primary focus is on ensuring data integrity in each entry, aligning with the existing asset display.

**Formatting Liability Data**

I'm now focusing on formatting the display of liabilities within the asset table, ensuring they are visually distinct yet consistent with the existing style. I plan to use the `formatCurrency` function for proper monetary display and incorporate clear labels to differentiate liabilities. My immediate goal is to create a unified table, effectively merging both assets and liabilities for streamlined viewing.


**Adjusting Component Aesthetics**

I'm now adapting the `AssetsLiabilities` component's styling and data retrieval. I'm removing the old mock data imports and implementing the `useFinance` hook to access accurate financial details, including the currency setting. I've updated class names for improved theme support (dark/light mode) consistency with other components for proper styling and visibility. My immediate focus is creating a unified, visually integrated table, effectively merging both assets and liabilities for streamlined viewing, alongside an updated table format for displaying interest rates.


**Refining Component Styling**

I'm now adapting the `AssetsLiabilities` component's styling and data retrieval. I'm removing the old mock data imports and implementing the `useFinance` hook to access accurate financial details, including the currency setting. I've updated class names for improved theme support (dark/light mode) consistency with other components for proper styling and visibility. My immediate focus is creating a unified, visually integrated table, effectively merging both assets and liabilities for streamlined viewing, alongside an updated table format for displaying interest rates.


**Enhancing Data Presentation**

I'm currently preparing the data for the new table, making sure each row is correctly displayed. I'm focusing on ensuring data integrity with precise formatting for all asset and liability items. I'm also ensuring the currency formatting adheres to the user's settings.


**Data Integrating Refinements**

I'm now integrating the summed values by asset type into the pie chart, including correct currency formatting. I'm ensuring consistency in colors and labels and adding a descriptive tooltip, which needs to display a proper currency format. I'm focusing on ensuring data integrity in the `allocationData` for an accurate and presentable visual representation.



**Improving Chart Data Flow**

I'm currently working to integrate the summed values by asset type into the pie chart, including correct currency formatting. I'm ensuring consistency in colors and labels and adding a descriptive tooltip, which needs to display a proper currency format. I'm focusing on ensuring data integrity in the `allocationData` for an accurate and presentable visual representation.


**Updating Table Data Display**

I'm currently preparing the data for the new table. I'm focusing on ensuring accurate currency formatting for all assets and liabilities. The table's structure is updated.


**Refining Table Display**

I'm currently preparing the data for the new table. I'm focusing on ensuring that the table correctly uses the user's currency settings, along with proper formatting for all asset and liability items. Furthermore, I've integrated `AssetIcon` to align the data presentation with the project's formatting conventions.

**Enhancing Debt Table**

I'm now adapting the table structure to include a section specifically for liabilities. I'll iterate through the `liabilities` array, displaying their details like name, type, and value formatted using `formatCurrency` and the user's settings. My main goal is to integrate these details seamlessly into the existing table, ensuring a clear and consistent presentation of both assets and debts.

I'm currently integrating the liabilities section into the table with focus on ensuring accuracy in the displayed debt totals. I'm leveraging the `useFinance` hook to fetch data. I'll format the total liabilities and display them in a visually appealing manner.

I'm leveraging distinct styling for due dates and financial figures, now using `formatCurrency` to provide clarity. My focus is on creating a comprehensive and easily understandable representation of liabilities.

I'm leveraging distinct styling for due dates and financial figures, now using `formatCurrency` to provide clarity. My focus is on creating a comprehensive and easily understandable representation of liabilities.


**Refactoring Table Structure**

I'm now adapting the table structure to integrate icons, improving visual clarity. I'll incorporate these icons from `lucide-react` into both the asset and liability entries for enhanced data representation. My focus is on ensuring a clean and consistent presentation of the data, with icons appropriately placed next to corresponding financial figures.

entries.description: Refactor AssetsLiabilities to use FinanceContext instead of removed mock data constants. Updated UI to support both light and dark themes consistent with other components.

payload.text:
I want you to improve and update its Budgets and Goals section. In the option of Budget and Goal, there is an option of Set a New Goal. But I want there to be added another button that says Create a Budget. And when someone clicks on Set New Goal, it should also have this date limit. So, like, you know, deadline of that goal that you want to achieve. And for the budgeting, make sure when someone clicks on the budget, create a new budget or create a budget option. There has to be enough options and there should be a lot of features for creating the budget. Like, which kind of budget you are trying to create? How much money do you want to put in this budget? And like, you know, most of the things that you can include to create a budget. So make sure nothing is left while creating a budget. And add this date feature into Set a New Goal.
Also in Liabilities & Debt Write full form for APR % field and vice versa in Assets & Allocation for APY %

**Analyzing UX Improvements**

I'm currently dissecting the "Budget & Goals" area, and I'm homing in on the need to separate "Budget" and "Goals" conceptually.  Right now, the single "Set New Goal" button is overloaded, encompassing both spending limits and aspirations. I'm strategizing how to untangle these functions to enhance clarity and provide users with a cleaner, more intuitive experience. The plan is to develop new distinct interaction points for these concepts.


**Develop Feature Set**

I've fleshed out the "Budget" feature significantly.  I envision a new "Create a Budget" button. "Set New Goal" now includes a deadline.  I'm also working on comprehensive budget fields (Period, Category, Alerts, Notes, Rollover).  Additionally, I'm updating the liabilities and assets UI to expand "APR" and "APY" for clarity. The update for Goals is being revised to include: Name, Target Amount, Current Amount (optional initial deposit), Deadline Date, and Notes, with a progress bar.


**Refining Data Structures**

I'm now zeroing in on the data structures. The plan is to create separate `Budget` and `Goal` interfaces to clarify our app's core concepts. My goal is for each to have its own unique fields and visualizations. The focus is now on the `Budget` interface and its related fields: Category, Amount Limit, Period, Recurring, Alert Threshold, and Description. I'm strategizing how to visualize the `Budget` as a bar graph comparing actual spend against the budget limit, with spend data sourced directly from transactions.


**Restructuring Data Storage**

I'm currently revising our data storage strategy. I'm focusing on the `storage.ts` file, and I've identified the need to separate `Budget` and `Goal` data. Currently, `GOALS` is a catch-all key.  I'm planning to introduce `aura_budgets` and `aura_savings_goals` to store them separately. I'll need to update the `FinanceContext.tsx` to include state and CRUD methods for both.  I will also start updates to the budget modal to include a recurring toggle and alert threshold.


**Outlining Data Flow**

I'm now outlining the data flow for the new budget and goal cards. The Budget card will pull spending versus limit directly from transaction data, while the Savings Goal card will use the entered current amount alongside a deadline countdown. I'm also changing "APR %" to "Annual Percentage Rate" and "APY %" to "Annual Percentage Yield" within the respective components. The new plan will focus on updating the "Budgeting.tsx" component and a complete overhaul of the budget forms.


**Enhancing Budget Types**

I'm zeroing in on enriching the budget options. I'm focusing on three budget types: "Category Cap," "Total Monthly Cap," and "One-off Project." I also plan to add "notes," "tags," and a "priority level" (High/Low) to enrich our current feature set. For "Goals," the deadline date is the most critical update. I've updated the plan to include refining budget types and goals.


**Revising Goal Metrics**

I'm now zeroing in on the goal visualization logic. My plan is to display progress with either a circular or bar graph, alongside a "Days Left" calculation to track progress toward the deadline. I'm also addressing the "Current Saved Amount" field in the `Goal` object, enabling manual updates to reflect progress for simplicity, bypassing the need for complex account linking. I'll begin by updating `types.ts`.


**Implementing Data Types**

I'm now focusing on a more granular approach to data modeling. I'm moving forward by introducing distinct `Budget` and `SavingsGoal` interfaces within `types.ts`, and removing the ambiguous `BudgetGoal`. I'm updating `services/storage.ts` to accommodate separate storage keys: `BUDGETS` and `SAVINGS_GOALS`. In `FinanceContext.tsx`, I'll implement state and CRUD operations for both data types to handle all the related UI components. Now the focus is to replace all code which uses `BudgetGoal` with the new, clearer interfaces.


**Reconciling UI Displays**

I'm now clarifying the UI for Assets & Allocation and Liabilities & Debt, as the user identified a duplication concern. Previously, I created `Assets.tsx` and `Liabilities.tsx` but the user's feedback suggests a misunderstanding regarding a lingering component. I'm reviewing `App.tsx` and the relevant routing to ensure distinct views and eliminate any lingering or redundant components.


**Addressing UI Concerns**

I'm now carefully reviewing user feedback about the Assets and Liabilities sections. The user noted a perceived duplication, which has me carefully reviewing the routing and component rendering. I'm checking the content and the navigation links to ensure everything is distinct and functional. I'm focusing on confirming that any prior copy-paste errors are resolved and making sure that the visual distinction is strong between these views to ensure clarity and improve the user experience.


**Confirming Screen Uniqueness**

I'm now zeroing in on verifying the distinctiveness of Assets & Allocation and Liabilities & Debt views. I'm focusing on `App.tsx` and the related routing, confirming that each view displays its dedicated content and navigation. I'll meticulously check for any residual components or shared sections to eliminate redundancy and strengthen visual separation, ensuring clarity in the user experience.


**Implementing Budget Logic**

I'm now focusing on implementing the backend logic for budget calculations, as well as the goal calculation logic. For budgets, I'm working to provide calculations based on monthly totals, spending per category, and the overall total. Goals will be calculated as a percentage of `currentAmount` over `targetAmount`, while displaying the days left and monthly savings needed to reach the target.  I'm updating `types.ts` to represent this, and ensuring `FinanceContext` provides the data. The next step is to examine and resolve an issue raised by the user about a possible duplicate screen rendering.


**Defining Next Steps**

I've completed the initial conceptualization of the required modifications and defined the specific steps to bring it all together. I'm focused on updating relevant files, including `types.ts`, `storage.ts`, `FinanceContext.tsx`, and `financeEngine.ts`. My key goal is a comprehensive overhaul of `Budgeting.tsx` to accommodate the new budget types. Now, I'm setting my sights on ensuring `Assets.tsx` and `Liabilities.tsx` are distinct and refined. I'll make sure the `Budgeting.tsx` component is fully functional. Finally, I will make the budgeting screen clean and feature-rich by employing a tabbed interface.

To fulfill your request, I will significantly expand the **Budgeting & Goals** module and refine the **Assets** and **Liabilities** naming conventions.

**Key Changes:**
1.  **Advanced Budgeting System**: Split "Budgeting" into two distinct engines: **Spending Budgets** (Expense Limiting) and **Savings Goals** (Wealth Accumulation).
2.  **Comprehensive "Create Budget" Modal**: Added deep customization including Budget Type (Category/Overall), Period (Monthly/Yearly), Alert Thresholds, and Auto-renewal settings.
3.  **Detailed "Set Goal" Modal**: Added the requested **Deadline Date**, along with initial deposit tracking, target visualization, and "Monthly Savings Needed" calculations.
4.  **UI/UX Polish**: Updated labels in Assets (APY - Annual Percentage Yield) and Liabilities (APR - Annual Percentage Rate) as requested.
5.  **Logic Updates**: Updated the data layer to support these new schemas.

Here is the implementation:

Added helpers to calculate budget spend and goal projections.
Updated UI with "Annual Percentage Yield" label.
Updated UI with "Annual Percentage Rate" label.
